title: Sentimental Value Editor on Crafting Bittersweet Moments in Joachim Trier's Oscar Contender
text: Oscar-nominated editor Olivier Bugge Coutté discussed his editing approach on Joachim Trier's Sentimental Value, which received a Best Film Editing nomination at the 2026 Oscars. Coutté explained how he balanced emotional weight with technical precision in the multi-generational family saga. The editor highlighted the importance of understanding character subtext and how editing decisions communicate emotion without dialogue. Sentimental Value's complex narrative structure, jumping between time periods and character perspectives, required meticulous editing to maintain clarity while preserving ambiguity. Coutté's work demonstrates how editing serves not just pacing but emotional resonance, particularly in intimate family moments where restraint becomes powerful. The article explores how Coutté's long collaboration with director Trier informed his editorial decisions and creative instincts.
